title: Circuit Minimization: Algebraic Rewriting
summary: Demonstrates minimisation of Boole function expressions using algebraic laws (idempotence, unification), listing the available methods and their trade-offs.
content_points: Minimisation reduces circuit cost (fewer gates), increases speed, and improves reliability.
Algebraic approach: use Boole laws to rewrite the DNF into a simpler equivalent expression.
Example: f = ABC + ABC + ABC + ABC → BC + AC + AB (using idempotence and unification).
Alternative methods: Karnaugh diagrams (visual), Quine-McCluskey (tabular), hybridisation.
learning_objectives: Apply idempotence and unification to simplify a DNF
Compare algebraic, Karnaugh, and Quine-McCluskey minimisation
